block_00: Take a look at the top news of the week
block_03: Elon Musk completed a $44 billion purchase of Twitter last week and took the company private, so Twitter's asset was delisted. The new owner immediately fired five executives, including the CEO. Musk has commandeered a staff of reportedly 50 Tesla employees. According to the news, workers at the electric carmaker are pressured to help with projects at Musk’s other companies for no additional pay.
block_07: New federal legislation in the USA has pumped new energy into the solar power market. First Solar announced plans to invest $1 billion to build a new solar panel plant shortly after the bill's signing – the share price raised by 16% this week
block_10: The health care provider lowered earnings estimates for the remainder of this year and into 2023, citing a lower volume of treatments that contributed to a decline in its Q3 profit margin. The share fell by 30% in one week.
